howItWorks.intro: This instant pot time calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Pressure Cook Time
rules.formula: IP Minutes = Slow Cooker Minutes x Conversion Ratio (varies by food)
rules.explanation: Pressure cooking is roughly 8-12x faster than slow cooking due to higher temperature and pressure.

howItWorks.outro: Cooking times and measurements can vary by equipment and altitude. Adjust based on your specific setup.

faq.q: What is the slow cooker to Instant Pot conversion?
faq.a: As a rough guide: 8 hours on low or 4 hours on high in a slow cooker equals about 25-30 minutes of pressure cooking. Always add at least 1 cup of liquid for the Instant Pot to build pressure.

faq.q: When do I use natural release vs quick release?
faq.a: Use natural release for large cuts of meat, beans, and foods that foam (prevents splattering). Use quick release for vegetables, chicken, and foods that overcook easily. Natural release takes 10-20 minutes and continues cooking food.
